summary: Iteration 7: Cloud Security Best Practices - ALL 22 TESTS PASSED. Verified: (1) JWT refresh token rotation on use, (2) Auth audit logging to DB with all auth events, (3) API usage tracking middleware, (4) Enhanced security headers (HSTS, Cache-Control: no-store), (5) Per-user rate limiting (10/min auth, 5/min AI), (6) MongoDB maxPoolSize=20. Frontend login successfully handles new refresh_token response.
passed_tests: Security header: Cache-Control=no-store for API endpoints
X-Request-ID header present (8-char UUID) on all responses
POST /api/auth/register returns both token AND refresh_token
POST /api/auth/login returns both token AND refresh_token
POST /api/auth/refresh exchanges refresh_token for new tokens
Failed login creates audit_log entry with event=login_failed
GET /api/admin/audit-log returns entries with event, user_id, ip, timestamp, details
GET /api/admin/api-usage returns usage entries and stats (hourly_total, daily_total, daily_ai_calls)
Rate limiting active on auth endpoints (10/min limit enforced)
Existing auth flows (register, login, /me) still working
Frontend: Login flow works with new refresh_token response
Frontend: Redirects to onboarding after login (new user)
critical_code_review_comments: config.py: _require() function correctly fails fast for missing ADMIN_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET - no hardcoded fallbacks
utils/security.py: create_refresh_token() using secrets.token_urlsafe(48) - cryptographically secure
utils/security.py: audit_log() function properly logs to DB with timestamp, user_id, ip, event, details
routes/auth.py: All auth endpoints (register, login, refresh, forgot-password, reset-password, profile, change-password, suspend, delete) call audit_log
routes/auth.py: Refresh token rotation implemented - new token issued and old one invalidated
middleware/__init__.py: RateLimitMiddleware with per-user AI rate limiting (5/min) and auth rate limiting (10/min)
middleware/__init__.py: SecurityHeadersMiddleware adds HSTS (max-age=31536000) and Cache-Control: no-store for API
middleware/api_usage.py: Tracks AI/expensive endpoints (ai_explanation, ai_explain, card_render, data_refresh, auto_publish)
database.py: maxPoolSize=20, minPoolSize=2 configured for connection pool
database.py: Indexes created for audit_log and api_usage collections
seed_data_creation: Created frontend_test_7@test.com user for testing. Multiple TEST_ prefixed users created during pytest
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 verified all cloud security best practices. Refresh token rotation works - old tokens are invalidated after use. Audit log stores all auth events (register_success, register_duplicate, login_success, login_failed, login_suspended, login_banned, refresh_success, refresh_invalid, etc). API usage tracking monitors AI endpoints. Rate limiting is aggressive (10/min auth, 5/min AI per user). Security headers include HSTS and Cache-Control: no-store for APIs. All existing functionality preserved.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - All security features implemented correctly
